verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent large complex (LLC) in which the LAP homodimer remains non-covalently (and partly covalently via disulfide) bound to the mature TGF-β1 dimer, and LTBP1/3/4 tethers this complex to ECM or to cell-surface integrins (αVβ6, αVβ8) and LRRC32/GARP or LRRC33/NRROS on the outer leaflet of regulatory T cells, platelets, and other cells. GARP/LRRC32 is a TM protein that covalently (disulfide) anchors the LAP-TGF-β1 pro-complex to the cell surface — this is a wash-resistant covalent link, not a reversible receptor equilibrium. This surface-tethered form is the target of clinical antibodies (e.g., anti-GARP:TGF-β1 bispecifics). However, this stable surface display is restricted to specific cell types (Tregs, platelets, macrophages) expressing GARP or NRROS and requires cell-type-specific co-expression, hence contextual rather than yes.
